steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is ayllus. “Ayllus” is a plural noun referring to multiple family clans, so the verb that follows must be plural to satisfy subject-verb agreement.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘is’: Singular present tense. Violates subject-verb agreement — a plural subject cannot take a singular verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘was’: Singular past tense. Also violates subject-verb agreement for the same reason. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘has been’: Singular present perfect. Still singular, still violates subject-verb agreement with the plural subject ayllus. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘were’: Plural past tense. Agrees in number with the plural subject ayllus — no agreement violation. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
